results.reasoning: A cites many specific statutes and FTC/FDA concepts but overstates risk and offers only generic remediation, while B is more concrete about one claim but lacks legal specificity and only moderately calibrates severity.

A cites several real authorities (FTC Act, FDCA/DSHEA, FDA structure/function and health-claim frameworks) but overstates/overgeneralizes enforcement risk and gives only limited concrete rewrite guidance; B is non-specific, incorrectly declares no risks, and offers no remediation.

A is highly specific on laws but overstates enforcement and offers little concrete editing guidance; B is more actionable with phrasing fixes but lacks explicit legal citations and only moderately calibrates severity.
